Aditya Birla Sun Life Insurance DigiShield Plan – Frequently Asked Questions
Section A
ABSLI DigiShield Plan – Plan Summary
1. What is ABSLI DigiShield Plan?
ABSLI DigiShield Plan is a new age term plan offering 10 unique plan options to suit the varied protection needs of customers and offers comprehensive coverage against life and health uncertainties.
2. What are the significant features of ABSLI DigiShield Plan?
ABSLI DigiShield Plan is a pure term insurance plan providing financial security to the family of the life insured in his absence.
... 10 Plan Options to suit your varied protection need;
Flexibility to choose term cover as per the need – coverage up to 100 years available
Ensure financial stability for the dependents in the absence of life insured with multiple payout options - lump-sum, monthly income or a combination of both.
Unique benefit – Receive Survival Income in a Term Plan – Guaranteed Monthly payouts post attaining Age 60 years for a stress-free retired life
Get all your premium back with Return of Premium (ROP) option in case you outlive the policy term

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
a Term Plan – Guaranteed Monthly payouts post attaining Age 60 years for a stress-free retired life
Get all your premium back with Return of Premium (ROP) option in case you outlive the policy term
Acceleration of base sum assured in the event of diagnosis of any Terminal Illness
Acceleration of Critical Illness sum assured through Accelerated Critical Illness Benefit option (ACI) where a lumpsum payout will be made to the life insured on diagnosis of any one of the covered 42 critical illness.
Include your loved one’s in the policy with Joint Life Protection option.
Additional protection through multiple Riders
3. What are the 10 Plan Options under this plan?
This plan offers unique 10 Plan Options as follows: -
Plan Option 1: Level Cover Option
o Pure Term cover: In case of unfortunate event, the nominee of the life insured will receive the sum assured as a lumpsum benefit
Plan Option 2: Increasing Cover Option
o Inflation proof term cover: The customer has an option to choose the percentage of yearly increase at the inception of policy.
o Once chosen the sum assured will continue to increase in the same percentage till the end of the policy term or death whichever is earlier.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).

o Once chosen the sum assured will continue to increase in the same percentage till the end of the policy term or death whichever is earlier.
o Death benefit as per the increased sum assured at the time of death will be payable.
Plan Option 3: Sum Assured Reduction Option
o Liability Protection cover: Align life insurance to outstanding liabilities; customer can avail higher cover during earning years and choose to reduce the cover post retirement age when liabilities are paid off.
o Complete flexibility – choose Sum Assured Reduction (25% or 50%) & Retirement Age (60/65/70/75 years)
Plan Option 4: Whole Life Option (Level Cover)
o Create Guaranteed Legacy corpus for your loved ones: Choose life cover for 100 years, on death, nominee will receive death benefit as lumpsum amount.
Plan Option 5: Whole Life Option (Sum Assured Reduction Cover)
o Ensure all your liabilities are protected for your entire life – stay protected till Age 100 years.
o Align life insurance to outstanding liabilities with flexibility to choose sum assured & retirement age.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).

o Align life insurance to outstanding liabilities with flexibility to choose sum assured & retirement age.
o Option to choose Sum Assured reduction ((25% or 50%)
o Option to choose Retirement Age (60/65/70/75)
Plan Option 6: Income Benefit
o USP “Second Term Plan”: Provide complete financial security to dependent family members.

8. What are the Maximum Sum Assured and Income limits allowed in the product? Please note that the product can be sourced with the following conditions:
Internal restriction of Minimum Sum Assured Rs. 50 Lakh remains unchanged.
Maximum Sum Assured capped at Rs. 20 crores
Option 8 will continue to be closed for sale.
All Premium Payment Terms and Policy Terms available under the product will be open for sale.
Smoker life sourcing is also allowed.
Minimum Income Criteria for both salaried and self-employed individuals will be:
o Rs 7.5 lakhs per annum for SA < 2 cr,
o Rs 10 lakhs per annum, for SA >= 2 cr
The customers, both salaried and self-employed with income 5 lac to less than 7.5 lac, will be offered Sum Assured of Rs. 50 lac with Maximum Maturity Age capped at 75 years
i.e. Policy Term can be maximum of (75 less Age at entry of LI).

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP). age 60Plan Option 10: Return of Premium (ROP)Regular Pay10 years55 years 11. What is the classification for lives to be assured under this plan? Depending upon the smoking status of the life insured, he/she will be classified as Non-Smoker or Smoker. The proposed life insured will be classified as Non-Smoker, if he/she has not consumed tobacco products in the last 12 months. This includes any nicotine products like cigarette, cigars, chewable tobacco or any other classified stimulants. 12. What are the differential premium rates offered under this plan? This plan offers preferential premium rates for all Female lives. Also, Policyholder get discounted premium rates for non – smoker status. 13. Describe the benefits offered under each plan option under this product?
Plan OptionOption nameOption Description
# 1Level Cover OptionUnder this option, In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, Nominee will be paid an absolute amount, equal to Sum Assured on

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
Plan OptionOption nameOption Description
# 1Level Cover OptionUnder this option, In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, Nominee will be paid an absolute amount, equal to Sum Assured on Death, in lump-sum.
# 2Increasing Cover OptionUnder this option the Sum Assured will increase every year during the Policy term. depending on chosen Sum Assured Escalation Rate selected by the Policyholder at inception. In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, the nominee will be paid an absolute amount, in lump-sum. The absolute amount is arrived at by increasing the Sum Assured by the chosen Sum Assured Escalation Rate, at each policy anniversary. Sum Assured Escalation Rate can be either 5% or 10% per annum.
# 3Sum Assured Reduction OptionUnder this option Policyholder will have an option to reduce the chosen Sum Assured by Sum Assured Reduction

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP). # 3Sum Assured Reduction OptionUnder this option Policyholder will have an option to reduce the chosen Sum Assured by Sum Assured Reduction Factor on attainment of Retirement Age. On exercising this option, Policy will continue with the reduced Sum Assured till the end of Policy Term. The reduction in Sum Assured will be effective from next Policy Anniversary. There will be no change in the Premium payable during the Premium Payment Term. In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, the nominee will be paid an absolute amount, in lump-sum. The absolute amount is the Sum Assured reduced by the chosen Sum Assured Reduction Factor at the chosen Retirement Age of the Life Insured. Sum Assured Reduction Factor can be either 50% or 25%. The Sum Assured Reduction Factor cannot be changed during the term of the policy. Retirement Age can be 60 years, 65 years, 70 years or 75 years. The Retirement Age should be at least 10 years more than the age of the Life Insured at inception of the policy and less than the maturity age.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP). 9Level Cover with Survival BenefitUnder this option, on survival of Life Insured till the policy anniversary falling after the attained age of 60 years, a Survival Benefit equal to 0.12% of Sum Assured, will be paid every month till the end of the Policy Term. In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, the nominee will be paid an absolute amount in lump-sum. The absolute amount is the Sum Assured less the Survival Benefit already paid.# 10Return of Premium (ROP)Under this option, on survival of Life Insured at the end of the Policy term, the total premiums, as defined under Maturity Benefit, will be paid to the Policyholder. In the unfortunate event of the death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, the nominee will be paid an absolute amount in lump-sum. 14. Can I change the Plan Option anytime during the term? No, Plan Option once chosen at inception; cannot be changed anytime during the Policy Term. 15. What is Sum Assured Escalation Rate? Sum Assured Escalation Rate is applicable under Plan Option 2 Increasing Cover Option, where the Sum

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).

Sum Assured Escalation Rate is applicable under Plan Option 2 Increasing Cover Option, where the Sum Assured increases by simple 5% p.a. or simple 10% p.a. as chosen by the Policyholder at inception.
16. Can I change the Sum Assured Escalation Rate during the Policy Term?
No, Sum Assured Escalation Rate chosen at inception cannot be changed anytime during the Policy Term. As the premium is calculated considering to the Sum Assured Escalation Rate and since the premiums cannot change during the Policy Term, the Sum Assured Escalation Rate once chosen cannot be changed thereafter.
17. What is Sum Assured Reduction Rate?
Sum Assured Reduction Rate is applicable under Plan Option 3 Sum Assured Reduction Option and Plan Option 5 Whole Life Option (Sum Assured Reduction Cover), where the Sum Assured reduced by 50% or 25% as chosen by the Policyholder at inception.
18. What is Retirement Age?
Retirement Age is applicable under Plan Option 3 Sum Assured Reduction Option and Plan Option 5 Whole Life Option (Sum Assured Reduction Cover).

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).

Retirement Age is applicable under Plan Option 3 Sum Assured Reduction Option and Plan Option 5 Whole Life Option (Sum Assured Reduction Cover). Policyholder has the option to select 60 years, 65 years, 70 years or 75 years as Retirement Age

19. Can I change the Sum Assured Reduction Rate or Retirement Age during the Policy Term? No, Sum Assured Reduction Rate or Retirement Age chosen at inception cannot be changed anytime during the Policy Term.
20. What is Income Benefit Period?
Income Benefit Period is applicable under Plan Option 6 Income Benefit, during which the monthly installments are paid to the Nominee after the death of the Life Insured. Policyholder has the option to select 10 years, 15 years or 20 years as Income Benefit Period at inception.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 Policyholder has the option to select 10 years, 15 years or 20 years as Income Benefit Period at inception.
Example: Hemant (aged 35) opts for Plan Option 6 Income Benefit with Sum Assured 1 crore with Policy Term of 50 years and Premium Payment Term of 10 years, Increasing Monthly Income by 5% p.a. and Income Benefit period of 10 years. In case of his death during 5th Policy Year, his nominee will be Rs. 1,25,000 per month starting from the next policy monthly anniversary on or after the date of death. 13th monthly installment will increase by 5% thus the Rs. 1,31,250 will be paid for next 12 months. Each year the monthly installment will keep increasing by simple Rs. 6,250 p.a.
21. Can I change the Income Benefit Period during the Policy Term?
No, Income Benefit Period chosen at inception cannot be changed anytime during the Policy Term.
22. Under Plan Option 7 Level Cover plus Income Benefit, is there any flexibility to select income period or percentage of income?
No, under Plan Option 7 Level Cover plus Income Benefit, monthly installment paid are fixed as 0.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
Cover plus Income Benefit, is there any flexibility to select income period or percentage of income?
No, under Plan Option 7 Level Cover plus Income Benefit, monthly installment paid are fixed as 0.5% of Sum Assured for fixed 120 months (10 years) and it cannot be changed anytime during the Policy Term.
23. Under Plan Option 9 Level Cover with Survival Benefit, when does the Survival Benefit will commence?
Under Plan Option 9 Level Cover with Survival Benefit, if the Life Insured survives till the policy anniversary falling after attaining age 60 years, 0.12% of Sum Assured per month will commence from the first policy monthiversary after the event and continue for each policy monthiversary till the end of the Policy Term or death of the Life Insured whichever is earlier.
Example: Deepak (aged 35) opts for Plan Option 9 Level Cover with Survival Benefit with Sum Assured 1 crore with Policy Term of 50 years and Premium Payment Term of To Age 60 Years (25 years). He will receive Rs. 12,000 per month from 26th Policy Year i.e. policy year falling after attaining age 60 years and will continue to receive monthly income till the end of Policy Term or till death whichever is earlier.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 policy year falling after attaining age 60 years and will continue to receive monthly income till the end of Policy Term or till death whichever is earlier.
24. What is the Terminal Illness benefit?
This is inbuilt benefit available under all Plan Options. In case life insured is diagnosed with a Terminal Illness up to an age of 80 years, while the policy is in force, 50% of the applicable Sum Assured on Death, subject to a maximum of Rs. 2 crores will be paid immediately as a lump-sum and future due premiums shall be waived off. On subsequent death of the Life Insured during the Policy Term, the Sum Assured on Death shall be reduced by the amount of Terminal Illness Benefit already paid. Terminal Illness Benefit shall only be payable on the first diagnosis of any Terminal Illness of the Life Insured during the policy term.
25. How will Terminal Illness Benefit work under Plan Option 2 Increasing Cover Option?
Under Plan Option 2 Increasing Cover Option, Terminal Illness Benefit payable will be 50% of applicable Sum Assured, subject to a maximum of Rs.

26. How will Terminal Illness Benefit work under Plan Option 6 Income Benefit?

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).

Under Plan Option 6 Income Benefit, Terminal Illness Benefit payable will be 50% of applicable Sum Assured, subject to a maximum of Rs. 2 crores. On the event of death once Terminal Illness claim is paid, monthly income as 1.25% of Sum Assured less Terminal Illness Benefit already paid, will be paid to the nominee. For this option, applicable Sum Assured is 1.25% x Sum Assured x Income Benefit Period (in months)
Example: Ganesh (aged 35) opts for Plan Option 6 Income Benefit for Sum Assured 1 crore with Policy Term of 50 years and Premium Payment Term of 10 years and Income Benefit of 10 years. His pay annual premium of Rs. 87,202 (incl taxes). His applicable Sum Assured is 1,50,00,000 (1,00,00,000 x 1.25% x 120 months)
Incase he is diagnosed with Terminal Illness during 30th Policy Year, Terminal Illness Benefit as 75,00,000 (50% of 1,50,00,000 subject max of 2,00,00,000) will be paid in lump sum. In case of death within 6months of Terminal Illness, monthly income as 31,250 (1.25% of balance Sum Assured i.e. 1,00,00,000 – 75,00,000 = 25,00,000) will be paid for 120 months to the nominee.

27. How will Terminal Illness Benefit work under Plan Option 7 Lump sum with Income Benefit?
Under Plan Option 7 Lump sum with Income Benefit, Terminal Illness Benefit payable will be 50% of applicable Sum Assured, subject to a maximum of Rs. 2 crores. On the event of death once Terminal Illness claim is paid, balance 50 % of Sum Assured will be paid in lump sum plus monthly income as 0.5% of Sum Assured will be paid for 120 months to the nominee.

34. How does Accelerated Critical Illness (ACI) Benefit work?
Under this benefit, ACI Sum Assured will be payable to Policyholder on the first diagnosis of any of the specified critical illnesses. Upon payment of the ACI Benefit Sum Assured, the Death Benefit will be reduced for the ACI benefit already paid, for the rest of the Policy Term and the Policy will continue provided all premiums are paid in full on due dates. Future premiums payable under the Policy for Death Benefit will also reduce proportionately. Once the ACI Sum Assured is paid on the first occurrence of any of the specified critical illness during the ACI Term, the ACI benefit will terminate.

36. What is the survival period applicable under ACI benefit?
There is no survival period applicable under ACI benefit. Hence Policyholder can claim ACI benefit in case in case of death even within 30 days of the diagnosis of any listed critical illnesses.
37. What is the waiting Period applicable under ACI benefit?
Waiting period of 90 days from the risk commencement date or revival date of the Policy, whichever is later.

Waiting period of 90 days from the risk commencement date or revival date of the Policy, whichever is later. Hence Policyholder cannot be claim ACI benefit in case of diagnosis of any listed critical illness within waiting period of 90 days.
38. What is Joint Life Protection?
Joint Life Protection is an optional benefit under which two lives can be covered on joint life basis as Primary Life Insured and Secondary Life Insured under the same policy. The sum assured applicable for the secondary life insured shall be equal to 50% of applicable sum assured for primary life insured. Currently spouse can be covered under Joint Life Option as Secondary Life Insured.
39. Joint Life Protection is available with which Plan Options? Joint Life Protection is available with Plan Option 1, 2 and 3 only.
40. What is Minimum and Maximum Sum Assured applicable for Secondary Life Insured under Joint Life Option? The sum assured applicable for the Secondary Life Insured is fixed as 50% of applicable sum assured for Primary Life Insured.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 The sum assured applicable for the Secondary Life Insured is fixed as 50% of applicable sum assured for Primary Life Insured. To avail Joint Life Option, the minimum Sum Assured of the Primary Life Insured should be Rs. 60 lakh.
41. Will Secondary Life Insured Sum Assured increase or reduce under Plan Option 2 and Plan Option 3? Yes, Secondary Life Insured Sum Assured will increase as per the chosen Sum Assured Escalation Rate under Plan Option 2 and in case of Plan Option 3, Secondary Life Insured Sum Assured will reduce based on the chosen Sum Assured Reduction Factor, at their respective retirement ages, based on the chosen Retirement Age for the Policy.
42. How does Joint Life Protection work?
If Policyholder has opted for Joint Life Protection, In case of death of the Primary Life Insured prior to the Secondary Life Insured: - Sum assured on death for Primary Life Insured will be paid to the Secondary Life Insured and the life cover for Secondary Life Insured will continue with the future premiums, if any, waived off.

Policyholder have to opt for this option at inception so it can be exercised by giving a written request to the Company within one year of the occurrence of specified events. 45. The first payment will become due on the first Policy monthiversary on or after the date of death. If Policyholder has chosen Increasing Monthly Income the monthly instalments will increase by 5% simple each year throughout the Income Benefit Term.

Any excess amount of Sum Assured on Death over the Effective Sum Assured, will be paid in lump-sum, immediately on death of Life Insured.

The Effective Sum Assured as on the date of death reflects any increase or reduction in Sum Assured chosen at inception of the Policy in accordance with the Plan Option chosen as defined below:

- For Option 3 & 5 – the Effective Sum Assured is same as the Sum Assured up to the first Policy Anniversary falling after the Retirement Age of the Life Insured and then the Sum Assured reduced by the Sum Assured Reduction Factor after the first Policy Anniversary falling after the Retirement Age of the Life Insured.

55. Can Nominee avail lump sum death benefit in installment or discounted value of future instalment in lump sum? No, since this plan offers choice of avail death benefit in lump sum or income or lump plus income thus Policyholder makes an informed choice while selecting the option to ensure the death benefit is paid to the nominee as required by them, hence there is no option for the Nominee to avail instalment of lump sum death benefit or discounted value of future instalment in lump sum.

57. What is the Survival Benefit under this plan?
For Plan Options 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10: There is no Survival Benefit payable For Plan Option 9: In the event the Life Insured survives till the Policy anniversary falling after the Life Insured attains an age of 60 years, Survival Benefit of 0.12% of Sum Assured per month is paid commencing with the first Policy Month Anniversary after the event and continuing for each Policy Moth Anniversary till the end of the Policy Term or death of the Life Insured whichever is earlier.

Premium Discontinuance and Revival
64. What happens if I do not pay my premium on time?
If you are unable to pay your policy premium by the due date, you will be given 30-day grace period, to pay your due premiums, during which your coverage will continue. however, In case of occurrence of death or Terminal Illness or critical illness during the grace period, the Company shall be entitled to deduct the unpaid Premium from the Benefits payable under the Policy.
65. What happens if I do not pay my premium within the grace period before the Policy has acquired surrender value? If your do not pay our premium within the grace period before the Policy has acquired surrender value, on expiry of the Grace Period, the Policy shall Lapse w.

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 If your do not pay our premium within the grace period before the Policy has acquired surrender value, on expiry of the Grace Period, the Policy shall Lapse w.e.f. the due date of unpaid premium, and all benefits under the Policy, including the insurance cover, shall cease and no benefits shall be payable, however, you will have the option to revive the Policy within 5 years from the due date of first unpaid premium.
66. What happens if I do not pay my premium within the grace period after the Policy has acquired surrender value? If you don’t pay the due premium during the Grace Period during the Premium Payment Term, on expiry of the grace period, the Policy shall become Reduced Paid Up (RPU) Policy. The Policy will however lapse for ACI risk coverage, if any, and no ACI benefit will be payable thereafter.
67. What is Reduced Paid Up (RPU) policy?
After the Policy has become RPU, the benefits payable will be amended as follows: The RPU Terminal Illness Benefit, RPU Sum Assured, RPU Effective Sum Assured and RPU Terminal Value shall be equal to the Terminal Illness Benefit, Sum Assured, Effective Sum Assured and Terminal Value respectively,

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
Illness Benefit, RPU Sum Assured, RPU Effective Sum Assured and RPU Terminal Value shall be equal to the Terminal Illness Benefit, Sum Assured, Effective Sum Assured and Terminal Value respectively, multiplied by the RPU Factor,
where the RPU Factor is the ratio of:
- The number of premium instalments paid to date; over
- The total number of premium instalments originally due for the Policy Term.

68. How can I revive my policy?
You can revive your Policy within a revival period of five years from the due date of first unpaid premium, subject to following conditions:
- Paying all outstanding premiums together with interest and/or late fees as declared by us from time to time;
- Providing evidence of Life Insured’s insurability satisfactory to us
- Revival of the Policy shall take effect only after revival of the Policy is approved by Us basis the Board approved underwriting Policy and communicated to

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
insurability satisfactory to us
- Revival of the Policy shall take effect only after revival of the Policy is approved by Us basis the Board approved underwriting Policy and communicated to you in writing.
Once the Policy has been revived, on the effective date of revival, all benefits will be restored to their full value. The monthly interest rate charged on unpaid premiums will be declared by ABSLI on June 1st of each calendar year and is determined as (x+1%)/12 rounded to the next 0.5%, where x is the base rate of the State Bank of India. The current applicable interest rate, as declared on June 1st, 2022, is 1% per month compounded annually. Any change in basis of determination of interest rate for revival can be done only after prior approval of the Authority. If a lapsed Policy is not revived within five years, the Policy shall be terminated, and no value is payable to you.
Termination
69. When will the Policy terminate?
The policy will be terminated at the earliest of:
the date of settlement of the Death Benefit or the date of settlement of death benefit on last surviving life in case of Joint Life Protection option; or
the date of payment of the surrender Benefit,

the date of payment of the surrender Benefit, if any; or

The date of maturity of the Policy; or
The date on which the sum assured on death has completely been paid out through ACI Benefit and/ or Terminal Illness claim.
The date on which we receive a free look cancellation request.

70. When will the ACI benefit terminate?
The ACI Benefit will terminate immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events, whichever is earliest At the end of ACI Term;
At the end of the Premium Payment Term of the Policy,
On attainment of age 70 years by the Life Insured
On payment of ACI benefit;
On death of the Life Insured;
If the Policy has not acquired a surrender value or unexpired risk premium value, the date on which the revival period ends;
The date on which we receive

such Medical Practitioner is not the policyholder’s spouse, father (including stepfather) or mother (including stepmother), son (including stepson), son’s wife, daughter, daughter’s husband, brother (including stepbrother) and sister (including stepsister) or Life Insured / policyholder under this policy and would be independent of the insurer.
72. What is Terminal Illness Exclusion under this plan?
The Life Insured will not be entitled to any Terminal Illness benefit if it is caused directly or indirectly due to or occasioned, accelerated or aggravated by intentional self-inflicted injury or attempted suicide, whether medically sane or insane.
73. What is ACI exclusion under this plan?
The Life Insured shall not be entitled to any ACI Benefits if the covered Critical Illness results either directly or indirectly from any of the following causes:
. Any Pre-Existing Disease. “Pre-existing Disease” means any condition, ailment, injury or disease:
a) That is/are diagnosed by a physician within 48 months prior to the effective date of the Policy issued by the insurer or its latest revival date, whichever is later; OR
b) For which medical advice

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 sedative or poison, except as prescribed by a registered medical practitioner acceptable to us;
Nuclear contamination, the radioactive, explosive or hazardous nature of nuclear fuel materials or property contaminated by nuclear fuel materials or accident arising from such nature;
. War, invasion, act of foreign enemy, hostilities (whether war be declared or not), armed or unarmed truce, civil war, mutiny, rebellion, revolution, insurrection, military or usurped power, riot or civil commotion, strikes.
. Taking part in any naval, military or air force operation during peace time.

74. What is the Suicide Exclusion under this plan?
In case the Life Insured, dies due to suicide within 12 months from the Risk Commencement Date or date of revival of the Policy, the Policy shall immediately terminate, and Company shall pay the following to the Nominee:
Where the Policy has acquired the Surrender Value/unexpired risk premium value, higher of Surrender Value or (Total Premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid excluding applicable taxes) till date of death.
Where the Policy hasn’t acquired the Surrender value/unexpired risk premium value, Total Premiums Paid plus underwriting extra premiums paid plus loadings for modal premiums paid excluding applicable taxes till date of death. For Joint Life Protection, the suicide exclusion described above applies in the event of earlier death of either the Primary Life Insured or the Secondary Life Insured and the life cover as mentioned in the Joint Life Protection section shall

The following details are about DigiShield Plan(DSP).
 For Joint Life Protection, the suicide exclusion described above applies in the event of earlier death of either the Primary Life Insured or the Secondary Life Insured and the life cover as mentioned in the Joint Life Protection section shall continue for the surviving Life Insured subject to the payment of reduced future premiums, if any.
Operational & Underwriting Guidelines
75. What application form is required for ABSLI DigiShield Plan?
Common Application Form – Major (E app) for ABSLI DigiShield Plan needs to be filled.
76. Can housewives be covered under this plan?
Yes, housewives can be covered under Joint Life Protection Option for sum assured up to 50% of husband’s life cover or 50 lakh, whichever is lower.
77. Can this plan be given to NRIs?
Yes, this plan can be offered to NRIs provided the client is residing in the standard residency country.
78. Is there any discount on this product for ABG employees?
Yes, 4% premium discount is applicable for employees of Aditya Birla Group companies buying their policies directly from ABSLI. No commission is payable on these policies.
